'''Ben Reynolds''' (born 15 January 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref name="Dons sign Reynolds on loan">{{cite web|url=http://www.doncasterrugbyleague.co.uk/article/42568/dons-sign-reynolds-on-loan|title=Dons sign Reynolds on loan|publisher=Doncaster RLFC|date=2015-04-04|accessdate=2016-09-15}}</ref>

==Background==
Reynolds was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]].

==Career==
Reynolds has previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], making his début in 2013. He spent most of the 2014 season on a dual registration contract with the [[York City Knights]] and was named as a joint winner (with [[James Saltonstall]]) of the Championship One Young Player of the Year award at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/championship/article/40699/kingstone-press-championships-awards- |title=Kingstone Press Championship Awards 2014 |website=Rugby League Championships |date=1 October 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141009015619/http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/championship/article/40699/kingstone-press-championships-awards- |archive-date=9 October 2014}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
In October 2014, Reynolds signed a contract with the [[Leigh Centurions]] for the 2015 season, with the [[Castleford Tigers]] having the first option to re-sign him for the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Taylor|first1=Mark|title=Centurions sign up youngster Reynolds|url=http://www.leighreporter.co.uk/sport/leigh-centurions/centurions-sign-up-youngster-reynolds-1-6927049|website=Leigh Reporter|accessdate=25 January 2015|date=31 October 2014}}</ref> He has spent time on loan at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref name="Reynolds joins Rams on season long loan">{{cite web|url=http://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/dewsbury-rams/reynolds-joins-rams-on-season-long-loan-1-7516734|title=Reynolds joins Rams on season long loan|publisher=Dewsbury Reporter|date=15 October 2015|accessdate=7 October 2016}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 10 August 2020 it was announced that Reynolds would join [[Toulouse Olympique]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=10 Aug 2020|accessdate=25 December 2020|title=Toulouse confirm signing of Ben Reynolds from Leigh|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-confirm-signing-of-ben-reynolds-from-leigh/}}</ref>

===Leigh (re-join)===
On 1 April 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Apr 2021|accessdate=2 April 2021|title=Leigh confirm Ben Reynolds signing|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-confirm-ben-reynolds-signing/}}</ref>

On 28 May 2022, Reynolds played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

On 17 July 2022, Reynolds scored two tries and kicked three goals in Leigh's 60-6 victory over [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/championship-rl-results-amazing-20-point-comeback-and-four-try-haul-for-former-hull-kr-man-56182/|title=Championship RL Results: Amazing 20-point comeback and four try haul for former Hull KR man|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>

In round 14 of the 2023 Super League season, Reynolds was sent off for punching Wakefield's David Fifita during Leigh's 30-4 victory at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leigh-leopards-ben-reynolds-wakefield-30151337/|title=Leigh Leopards shrug off Ben Reynolds red card to wallop Wakefield Trinity|website=www.mirror.co.uk}}</ref>

On 12 August 2023, Reynolds played for Leigh in their Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

Reynolds played 24 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 19 October 2023, it was reported he had signed a two-year deal to join [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 October 2023|title=Leigh Leopards ace Ben Reynolds sees Championship move confirmed ahead of 2024 as he joins boyhood club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-ace-ben-reynolds-sees-championship-move-confirmed-ahead-of-2024-as-he-joins-boyhood-club}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 23 February 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 February 2024|title=Ben Reynolds: Hull KR sign ex-Leigh half-back from Featherstone|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68384325}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers (DR)===
On 12 April 24 he returned to [[Featherstone Rovers]] albeit on DR loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 April 2024|title=Hull KR's Ben Reynolds returns to Featherstone Rovers on dual-registration following Super League move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-krs-ben-reynolds-returns-to-featherstone-rovers-on-dual-registration-following-super-league-move/}}</ref>

===Hull F.C. (loan)===
On 9 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 May 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Castleford Tigers half-back makes Hull KR loan move as Ben Reynolds heads to Hull FC|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-danny-richardson-hull-kr-ben-reynolds-hull-fc-loan-moves}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers (re-join)===
On 8 July 2024 it was reported that he had re-signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] with immediate effect on an 18-month deal, after less than six months with [[Hull KR]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=8 July 2024|author=Arindam Rej|title=Hull KR's blunt Ben Reynolds exit message tells tale with departure confirmed|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-krs-blunt-ben-reynolds-9396958}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 29 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Featherstone Rovers]] early, amidst their financial troubles, to join [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the 2026 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Ex-Leigh stalwart makes cross-Championship switch ahead of 2026 as reunion with former coach confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-reynolds-joins-batley-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>
